description_long: This object is a rectangular jersey barrier constructed primarily from a wood-like material, featuring a series of parallel grooves or slots running lengthwise along its top surface. The sides are solid and slightly angled, giving the barrier a trapezoidal profile when viewed from the ends. Metal handles or rails are attached to both shorter ends, with one side showing a curved metal loop extending outward, likely for ease of movement or installation. The wood surface displays a natural grain pattern with some darker streaks, and the overall finish appears matte and slightly rough. There are no visible reflective strips, warning signs, or significant damage, but there is faint writing or marking on the end faces. This barrier is designed to be placed on roadsides or medians to control vehicle or pedestrian access.
description: A rectangular, wood-like jersey barrier with parallel grooves on top and metal handles on each end, featuring a matte, slightly rough finish and faint end markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat, grooved top surface and both metal handles partially visible on either side.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the trapezoidal profile, a single metal handle, and faint writing or marking on the end face.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the grooved top and the complete extension of the curved metal handles.
description_view_3: Opposite end view similar to view 1, with the metal handle and faint end markings visible.
